[[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ({{ill|Mikumo-Ihara Site|ja|三雲・井原遺跡}} Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} archaeological site located in Mikumo, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|ja|糸島市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|lt=Ito Kingdom|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku}}<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February {{ill|1822|ja|文政5th year }}, Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In {{ill|1974|ja|昭和49th year }}, a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Site|ja|平原遺跡}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors|ja|異体字銘帯鏡}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013

revision.text:
[[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ({{ill|Mikumo-Ihara Site|ja|三雲・井原遺跡|12=draft}} Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} archaeological site located in Mikumo, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|ja|糸島市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|12=draft}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|lt=Ito Kingdom|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|12=draft}}<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February {{ill|1822|ja|文政5th year|12=draft}}, Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In {{ill|1974|ja|昭和49th year|12=draft}}, a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=draft}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Site|ja|平原遺跡|12=draft}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=draft}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors|ja|異体字銘帯鏡|12=draft}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=draft}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013
== See Also == * {{ill|Itokoku|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|12=draft}} * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]
== External Links == * [http://inoues.net/ruins/mikumoshouji.html Mikumo-Minami Shōji and Ihara Yari Sites]

[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Yayoi period sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Japanese Kofuns]] [[Category:Yayoi Period Sites]] [[Category:Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sanyumo Minamikoji Ruins]] [[Category:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] [[sn:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]] [[sn:ikumo Nanokoji Site]] [[en:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mikumo Nanokoji Site}}

[[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ({{ill|Mikumo-Ihara Site|ja|三雲・井原遺跡|12=draft}} Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} archaeological site located in Mikumo, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|ja|糸島市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|12=draft}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|lt=Ito Kingdom|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|12=draft}}<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February {{ill|1822|ja|文政5th year|12=draft}}, Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In {{ill|1974|ja|昭和49th year|12=draft}}, a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=draft}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Site|ja|平原遺跡|12=draft}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=draft}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors|ja|異体字銘帯鏡|12=draft}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=draft}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * {{ill|Itokoku|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|12=draft}} * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]

[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Yayoi period sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Japanese Kofuns]] [[Category:Yayoi Period Sites]] [[Category:Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sanyumo Minamikoji Ruins]] [[Category:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] [[sn:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]] [[sn:ikumo Nanokoji Site]] [[en:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mikumo Nanokoji Site}}

[[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ({{ill|Mikumo-Ihara Site|ja|三雲・井原遺跡|12=draft}} Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} archaeological site located in Mikumo, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|ja|糸島市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|12=draft}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|lt=Ito Kingdom|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|12=draft}}<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February {{ill|1822|ja|文政5th year|12=draft}}, Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In {{ill|1974|ja|昭和49th year|12=draft}}, a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=draft}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Site|ja|平原遺跡|12=draft}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=draft}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors|ja|異体字銘帯鏡|12=draft}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=draft}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * {{ill|Itokoku|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|12=draft}} * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]
== External Links == * [http://inoues.net/ruins/mikumoshouji.html Mikumo-Minami Shōji and Ihara Yari Sites]

== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * {{ill|Itokoku|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|12=draft}} * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]

[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Yayoi period sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Japanese Kofuns]] [[Category:Yayoi Period Sites]] [[Category:Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sanyumo Minamikoji Ruins]] [[Category:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] [[sn:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]] [[sn:ikumo Nanokoji Site]] [[en:Mikumo Nanokoji Site]] {{citation needed|Mikumo Nanokoji Site}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

[[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ({{ill|Mikumo-Ihara Site|ja|三雲・井原遺跡|12=draft}} Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} archaeological site located in Mikumo, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|ja|糸島市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|12=draft}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|lt=Ito Kingdom|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|12=draft}}<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February {{ill|1822|ja|文政5th year|12=draft}}, Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In {{ill|1974|ja|昭和49th year|12=draft}}, a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=draft}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Site|ja|平原遺跡|12=draft}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=draft}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors|ja|異体字銘帯鏡|12=draft}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=draft}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * {{ill|Itokoku|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|12=draft}} * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]
== External Links == * [http://inoues.net/ruins/mikumoshouji.html Mikumo-Minami Shōji and Ihara Yari Sites]

38 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:三雲南小路遺跡 up to rev 97848018
revision.text:
[[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|三雲南小路遺跡（[[三雲・井原遺跡]]南小路地区）。]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = 三雲南小路遺跡| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|位置}}| float =right}} '''三雲南小路遺跡'''（みくもみなみしょうじいせき）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲に所在する[[弥生時代]]の[[遺跡]]。「[[三雲・井原遺跡]]」の一部（三雲・井原遺跡南小路地区）として国の[[史跡]]に指定されており<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html 「史跡一覧」（糸島市サイト）]</ref>、[[細石神社]]の裏手に所在する。[[甕棺]]2基からなる埋葬主体部と周溝をもつ一辺30メートルの[[弥生墳丘墓]]で、古代[[伊都国]]の王墓と目されている<ref name=糸島市>{{Cite web|和書|author=地域振興部文化課|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=伊都国の王都「三雲・井原遺跡」が国史跡に指定されました。|work=糸島市|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>。
== 概要 == [[文政]]5年（1822年）2月、三苫清四郎が住宅の土塀を築こうと南小路の畠の土を取ろうとして偶然発見したという<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=糸島地区社会教育振興協議会文化協会|title=伊都国遺跡ガイドブック|publisher=糸島新聞社|date=2001-4-30|page=52|isbn=}}</ref>。後の1974年（昭和49年）の再調査の時に「2号甕棺」が発見された。平成の学術調査で周溝をもつことが判明し、現在は「'''方形周溝墓'''で、甕棺を2器を添える様にして設置した墓」である、とされる。[[甕棺]]の形式は「立岩式古段階（弥生時代中期中頃）」の形状をもつ。西側の周溝に祭祀跡とみられる痕跡があり、東側の[[高祖山]]系の山並みとの関連性がうかがえる。これは後の時代の[[平原遺跡]]1号墓（平原弥生古墳）に通じるものであろう<ref>「三雲遺跡 南小路地区編」福岡県文化財調査報告書、第69集 福岡県教育委員会1985</ref>。
最新の研究では、この三雲南小路遺跡と、[[春日市]]の[[須玖岡本遺跡]]の巨石下甕棺墓とは同一規模の構造を呈している可能性が示唆されている<ref>「自然と遺跡からみた福岡の歴史」福岡市史編集委員会2013</ref>。
== 副葬品<ref>「柳園古器略考」[[青柳種信]]著、「三雲遺跡 南小路地区編」福岡県文化財調査報告書、第69集 福岡県教育委員会1985</ref> == '''1号甕棺''' :甕棺外部 * 銅剣 1 * 銅戈 1 * 朱入小壺 1 :甕棺内部 * 銅矛 2 * 銅鏡（[[前漢鏡]]） 31面以上 * ガラス璧(瑠璃璧)破片 8個以上 * ガラス勾玉 3個 * ガラス管玉 60個以上 * 金銅製四葉飾金具 8個以上 ※銅鏡は27.3センチメートルから16.0センチメートルの物で『[[異体字銘帯鏡|連弧文銘帯鏡]]』が 26面以上と大半を占める。
'''2号甕棺''' * 銅鏡（前漢鏡） 22面以上 * ガラス垂飾（瑠璃璧の破片の再利用品？） 1 * 勾玉 13個（硬玉製 1、ガラス製 12） ※銅鏡は11.4センチメートルから6.0センチメートルの小型鏡で、『連弧文「日光」銘鏡』が 16面以上と大半を占める。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *「柳園古器略考」青柳種信著 *「悲劇の金印」[[原田大六]]著 *「三雲遺跡 南小路地区編」福岡県文化財調査報告書、第69集 福岡県教育委員会1985 *「自然と遺跡からみた福岡の歴史」福岡市史編集委員会2013 ==関連項目== *[[伊都国]] *[[三雲・井原遺跡]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://inoues.net/ruins/mikumoshouji.html 三雲南小路・井原鑓遺跡]
{{考古学}} {{日本の文化財}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:みくもみなみしようしいせき}} [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]] [[Category:弥生時代の遺跡]] [[Category:福岡県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

revision.text:
[[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|三雲南小路遺跡（[[三雲・井原遺跡]]南小路地区）。]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = 三雲南小路遺跡| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|位置}}| float =right}} '''三雲南小路遺跡'''（みくもみなみしょうじいせき）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲に所在する[[弥生時代]]の[[遺跡]]。「[[三雲・井原遺跡]]」の一部（三雲・井原遺跡南小路地区）として国の[[史跡]]に指定されており<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html 「史跡一覧」（糸島市サイト）]</ref>、[[細石神社]]の裏手に所在する。[[甕棺]]2基からなる埋葬主体部と周溝をもつ一辺30メートルの[[弥生墳丘墓]]で、古代[[伊都国]]の王墓と目されている<ref name=糸島市>{{Cite web|和書|author=地域振興部文化課|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=伊都国の王都「三雲・井原遺跡」が国史跡に指定されました。|work=糸島市|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>。
== 概要 == [[文政]]5年（1822年）2月、三苫清四郎が住宅の土塀を築こうと南小路の畠の土を取ろうとして偶然発見したという<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=糸島地区社会教育振興協議会文化協会|title=伊都国遺跡ガイドブック|publisher=糸島新聞社|date=2001-4-30|page=52|isbn=}}</ref>。後の1974年（昭和49年）の再調査の時に「2号甕棺」が発見された。平成の学術調査で周溝をもつことが判明し、現在は「'''方形周溝墓'''で、甕棺を2器を添える様にして設置した墓」である、とされる。[[甕棺]]の形式は「立岩式古段階（弥生時代中期中頃）」の形状をもつ。西側の周溝に祭祀跡とみられる痕跡があり、東側の[[高祖山]]系の山並みとの関連性がうかがえる。これは後の時代の[[平原遺跡]]1号墓（平原弥生古墳）に通じるものであろう<ref>「三雲遺跡 南小路地区編」福岡県文化財調査報告書、第69集 福岡県教育委員会1985</ref>。
最新の研究では、この三雲南小路遺跡と、[[春日市]]の[[須玖岡本遺跡]]の巨石下甕棺墓とは同一規模の構造を呈している可能性が示唆されている<ref>「自然と遺跡からみた福岡の歴史」福岡市史編集委員会2013</ref>。
== 副葬品<ref>「柳園古器略考」[[青柳種信]]著、「三雲遺跡 南小路地区編」福岡県文化財調査報告書、第69集 福岡県教育委員会1985</ref> == '''1号甕棺''' :甕棺外部 * 銅剣 1 * 銅戈 1 * 朱入小壺 1 :甕棺内部 * 銅矛 2 * 銅鏡（[[前漢鏡]]） 31面以上 * ガラス璧(瑠璃璧)破片 8個以上 * ガラス勾玉 3個 * ガラス管玉 60個以上 * 金銅製四葉飾金具 8個以上 ※銅鏡は27.3センチメートルから16.0センチメートルの物で『[[異体字銘帯鏡|連弧文銘帯鏡]]』が 26面以上と大半を占める。
'''2号甕棺''' * 銅鏡（前漢鏡） 22面以上 * ガラス垂飾（瑠璃璧の破片の再利用品？） 1 * 勾玉 13個（硬玉製 1、ガラス製 12） ※銅鏡は11.4センチメートルから6.0センチメートルの小型鏡で、『連弧文「日光」銘鏡』が 16面以上と大半を占める。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *「柳園古器略考」青柳種信著 *「悲劇の金印」[[原田大六]]著 *「三雲遺跡 南小路地区編」福岡県文化財調査報告書、第69集 福岡県教育委員会1985 *「自然と遺跡からみた福岡の歴史」福岡市史編集委員会2013 ==関連項目== *[[伊都国]] *[[三雲・井原遺跡]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211693]] Wikidata name: Mikumo-Minamishōji Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ([[:en:Mikumo Iwara|Mikumo-Ihara Site]] Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] archaeological site located in Mikumo, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Ito Kingdom]]<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February [[:en:1822|1822]], Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In [[:en:1974|Showa 49]], a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]] * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]
== External Links == * [http://inoues.net/ruins/mikumoshouji.html Mikumo-Minami Shōji and Ihara Yari Sites]

The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Ito Kingdom]]<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February [[:en:1822|1822]], Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In [[:en:1974|Showa 49]], a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==

<!--Mikumo Nanokoji Site--><!--Mikumo Nanokoji Site--><!--Mikumo Nanokoji Site--><!--Mikumo Nanokoji Site--><!--Mikumo Nanokoji Site--><!--Mikumo Nanokoji Site--> [[Category:Yayoi period ruins]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] [[Category:National historic sites in Fukuoka prefecture]]
[[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Mikumo-Minamishōji Site}} {{wikidata link|Q17211693}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17211693]] Wikidata name: Mikumo-Minamishōji Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ([[:en:Mikumo Iwara|Mikumo-Ihara Site]] Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] archaeological site located in Mikumo, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Ito Kingdom]]<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February [[:en:1822|1822]], Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In [[:en:1974|Showa 49]], a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]] * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]

== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]] * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17211693]] Wikidata name: Mikumo-Minamishōji Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ([[:en:Mikumo Iwara|Mikumo-Ihara Site]] Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] archaeological site located in Mikumo, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Ito Kingdom]]<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February [[:en:1822|1822]], Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In [[:en:1974|Showa 49]], a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]] * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]
== External Links == * [http://inoues.net/ruins/mikumoshouji.html Mikumo-Minami Shōji and Ihara Yari Sites]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17211693]] Wikidata name: Mikumo-Minamishōji Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ([[:en:Mikumo Iwara|Mikumo-Ihara Site]] Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] archaeological site located in Mikumo, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Ito Kingdom]]<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February [[:en:1822|1822]], Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In [[:en:1974|Showa 49]], a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]] * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]
== External Links == * [http://inoues.net/ruins/mikumoshouji.html Mikumo-Minami Shōji and Ihara Yari Sites]

These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.

== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]] * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17211693]] Wikidata name: Mikumo-Minamishōji Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ([[:en:Mikumo Iwara|Mikumo-Ihara Site]] Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] archaeological site located in Mikumo, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Ito Kingdom]]<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February [[:en:1822|1822]], Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In [[:en:1974|Showa 49]], a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]] * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17211693]] Wikidata name: Mikumo-Minamishōji Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ([[:en:Mikumo Iwara|Mikumo-Ihara Site]] Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] archaeological site located in Mikumo, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Ito Kingdom]]<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February [[:en:1822|1822]], Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In [[:en:1974|Showa 49]], a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]] * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]
== External Links == * [http://inoues.net/ruins/mikumoshouji.html Mikumo-Minami Shōji and Ihara Yari Sites]

It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Ito Kingdom]]<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February [[:en:1822|1822]], Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.

Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
