[[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Mikumo-Minamishōji Site}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17211693}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17211693]] Wikidata name: Mikumo-Minamishōji Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ([[:en:Mikumo Iwara|Mikumo-Ihara Site]] Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] archaeological site located in Mikumo, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Ito Kingdom]]<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February [[:en:1822|1822]], Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In [[:en:1974|Showa 49]], a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==

It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Ito Kingdom]]<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February [[:en:1822|1822]], Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In [[:en:1974|Showa 49]], a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==

== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]] * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]

== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]] * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Mikumo-Minamishōji Site}} {{wikidata link|Q17211693}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:National historic sites in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] [[Category:Yayoi period ruins]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17211693]] Wikidata name: Mikumo-Minamishōji Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ([[:en:Mikumo Iwara|Mikumo-Ihara Site]] Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] archaeological site located in Mikumo, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Ito Kingdom]]<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February [[:en:1822|1822]], Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In [[:en:1974|Showa 49]], a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the [[:en:Suguokamoto_Site|Suguokamoto Site]] burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are [[:en:Inscribed mirror|Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors]], comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]] * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]
== External Links == * [http://inoues.net/ruins/mikumoshouji.html Mikumo-Minami Shōji and Ihara Yari Sites]

== Overview == In February {{ill|1822|en|1822|WD=Q6983|lt=1822}}, Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=Showa 49}}, a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the {{ill|Suguokamoto_Site|en|Suguokamoto_Site|WD=Q11473236|lt=Suguokamoto Site}} burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.

Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|en|Inscribed mirror|WD=Q112835927|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.

Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17211693]] Wikidata name: Mikumo-Minamishōji Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ({{ill|Mikumo Iwara|en|Mikumo Iwara|WD=Q109357954|lt=Mikumo-Ihara Site}} Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} archaeological site located in Mikumo, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima, Fukuoka}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|WD=Q10885162|lt=Ito Kingdom}}<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February {{ill|1822|en|1822|WD=Q6983|lt=1822}}, Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=Showa 49}}, a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the {{ill|Suguokamoto_Site|en|Suguokamoto_Site|WD=Q11473236|lt=Suguokamoto Site}} burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|en|Inscribed mirror|WD=Q112835927|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013 == See Also == * {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|WD=Q10885162|lt=Itokoku}} * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]
== External Links == * [http://inoues.net/ruins/mikumoshouji.html Mikumo-Minami Shōji and Ihara Yari Sites]

Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the {{ill|Suguokamoto_Site|en|Suguokamoto_Site|WD=Q11473236|lt=Suguokamoto Site}} burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|en|Inscribed mirror|WD=Q112835927|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]]
[[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ({{ill|Mikumo Iwara|en|Mikumo Iwara|WD=Q109357954|lt=Mikumo-Ihara Site}} Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} archaeological site located in Mikumo, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima, Fukuoka}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|WD=Q10885162|lt=Ito Kingdom}}<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February {{ill|1822|en|1822|WD=Q6983|lt=1822}}, Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=Showa 49}}, a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the {{ill|Suguokamoto_Site|en|Suguokamoto_Site|WD=Q11473236|lt=Suguokamoto Site}} burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|en|Inscribed mirror|WD=Q112835927|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Mikumo-Minamishōji Site}} {{wikidata link|Q17211693}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:National historic sites in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] [[Category:Yayoi period ruins]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Mikumo-Minamishōji Site]][[ja:三雲南小路遺跡]]
[[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ({{ill|Mikumo Iwara|en|Mikumo Iwara|WD=Q109357954|lt=Mikumo-Ihara Site|1=Mikumo Iwara}} Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} archaeological site located in Mikumo, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima, Fukuoka|1=Itoshima}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|WD=Q10885162|lt=Ito Kingdom|1=Itokoku}}<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February {{ill|1822|en|1822|WD=Q6983|lt=1822|1=1822}}, Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=Showa 49|1=1974}}, a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the {{ill|Suguokamoto_Site|en|Suguokamoto_Site|WD=Q11473236|lt=Suguokamoto Site|1=Suguokamoto Site}} burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|en|Inscribed mirror|WD=Q112835927|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors|1=Inscribed mirror}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013
== See Also == * {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|WD=Q10885162|lt=Itokoku|1=Itokoku}} * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]
== External Links == * [http://inoues.net/ruins/mikumoshouji.html Mikumo-Minami Shōji and Ihara Yari Sites]

== Overview == In February {{ill|1822|en|1822|qid=Q6983|lt=1822|1=1822}}, Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In {{ill|1974|en|1974|qid=Q2478|lt=Showa 49|1=1974}}, a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the {{ill|Suguokamoto_Site|en|Suguokamoto_Site|qid=Q11473236|lt=Suguokamoto Site|1=Suguokamoto Site}} burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|en|Inscribed mirror|qid=Q112835927|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors|1=Inscribed mirror}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * "Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi * "The Tragedy of the Golden Seal" by Dairoku Harada * "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985 * "History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013
== See Also == * {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|qid=Q10885162|lt=Itokoku|1=Itokoku}} * [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]]

revision.text:
[[File:Mikumo-Minamishouji Site, zenkei.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Mikumo Minamishouji Site ({{ill|Mikumo Iwara|en|Mikumo Iwara|qid=Q109357954|lt=Mikumo-Ihara Site|1=Mikumo Iwara}} Minamishouji District).]] {{Coord|33|32|12.1|N|130|14|29.6|E|display=title}} {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1| lat_deg = 33| lat_min = 32| lat_sec = 12.1| lon_deg = 130| lon_min = 14| lon_sec = 29.6| label = Mikumo Minamishouji Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 250| caption = {{Center|Location}}| float =right}} '''Mikumo Minamishouji Site''' (三雲南小路遺跡) is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} archaeological site located in Mikumo, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|qid=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima, Fukuoka|1=Itoshima}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mikumo-Iwara_Site|Mikumo-Iwara Site]], which has been designated as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/030/bunkazai3.html "List of Historic Sites" (Itoshima City Website)]</ref>. The site is located behind {{ill|Saisa Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisa Shrine|qq=}} and includes a square Kofun (30 meters on each side) with two jar burials and a surrounding moat. It is considered a royal tomb of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|qid=Q10885162|lt=Ito Kingdom|1=Itokoku}}<ref name=Itoshima>{{Cite web|author=Department of Regional Promotion, Cultural Division|url=https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/170/20171129102855.html|title=The Royal Capital of Ito Kingdom: "Mikumo-Ihara Site" Designated as a National Historic Site|work=Itoshima City|accessdate=2022-11-19}}</ref>.
== Overview == In February {{ill|1822|en|1822|qid=Q6983|lt=1822|1=1822}}, Seishiro Mitoma accidentally discovered the site while excavating soil from a field at Minamishouji for building walls<ref>{{Cite book|author=Itoshima District Social Education Promotion Council|title=Ito Kingdom Archaeological Guidebook|publisher=Itoshima Newspaper|date=2001-4-30|page=52}}</ref>. In {{ill|1974|en|1974|qid=Q2478|lt=Showa 49|1=1974}}, a re-excavation revealed the "second jar burial." Academic investigations during the Heisei period confirmed the presence of a surrounding moat, identifying the site as a "square-moat Kofun" containing two jar burials. The jars are classified as "Tateiwa-style early-phase" from the middle Yayoi period. Ritual traces have been observed in the western moat, with potential links to the {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takazusayama mountain range|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takazusayama mountain range|qq=}} to the east. These characteristics anticipate the features seen in the later {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun 1<ref>"Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref>.
Recent studies suggest that the Mikumo Minamishouji Site and the {{ill|Suguokamoto_Site|en|Suguokamoto_Site|qid=Q11473236|lt=Suguokamoto Site|1=Suguokamoto Site}} burial with a large jar beneath a megalith in Kasuga City may have similar structural scales<ref>"History of Fukuoka from Nature and Archaeology," Fukuoka City Historical Compilation Committee, 2013.</ref>.
== Grave Goods<ref>"Rikuen Old Artifacts Analysis" by Munetada Aoyagi, "Mikumo Site: Minamishouji District," Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Property Survey Report No. 69, Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education, 1985.</ref> == '''Jar Burial 1''' * '''Outside the jar''' ** 1 bronze sword ** 1 bronze halberd ** 1 small vermilion-stained jar * '''Inside the jar''' ** 2 bronze spears ** 31 or more bronze mirrors ({{ill|Han-style mirror|lt=Han-style mirror|lt=Han mirrors|ja|前漢鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢鏡}}) ** 8 or more fragments of glass bi disks (lapis lazuli) ** 3 glass magatama beads ** Over 60 glass tubular beads ** 8 or more gold-co11er four-leaf decorative fittings ** '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 27.3 cm to 16.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|en|Inscribed mirror|qid=Q112835927|lt=Linked arc-patterned inscribed band mirrors|1=Inscribed mirror}}, comprising 26 or more specimens.
'''Jar Burial 2''' * 22 or more bronze mirrors (Han mirrors) * 1 glass pendant (possibly reused fragments of a lapis lazuli bi disk) * 13 magatama beads (1 jadeite, 12 glass) * '''Bronze mirrors''': Sizes range from 11.4 cm to 6.0 cm. Most are {{ill|Inscribed mirror|lt=Linked arc-patterned "Sunlight" inscribed mirrors|ja|連弧文「日光」銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscribed mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, comprising 16 or more specimens.
